title: AI Media Rights, Consent & Provenance Registry
description: Per provider and service surface: commercial-use terms, output ownership, training-use defaults, voice-clone consent mechanics, provenance and watermark claims, and indemnity positions for AI video, speech, and music APIs, with dated primary sources, quoted evidence, and explicit verification status. Descriptive records, not legal advice; confirm against the linked terms.
citation: Vorp Labs, AI Media Rights, Consent & Provenance Registry (as of 2026-07-15), https://vorplabs.com/models/media-rights

records.termsDocument: OpenAI Services Agreement; OpenAI Service Terms

commercialUse.summary: Commercially usable subject to law, policy, and third-party rights; the agreement assigns Output to the customer but contains no dedicated commercial-use clause.

outputOwnership.summary: Assigned: the customer owns Output to the extent permitted by law, and OpenAI assigns its right, title, and interest in it. Similar output may be generated for other customers.

trainingUseDefault.summary: Not used for training by default; API customer content trains models only if the customer explicitly opts in.

provenanceClaims.summary: Provider claim: Sora videos carry C2PA metadata plus visible and invisible provenance signals. The claim is Sora-wide, not an API response-schema guarantee, and Vorp Labs has not yet tested output behavior.

indemnity.summary: Output-IP indemnity for API customers, with exclusions for known or likely infringement, ignored safeguards, modification or combination, missing Input rights, trademark trade use, and Third Party Offering content.

factChecks.label: Output assignment
factChecks.value: Customer owns all Output; OpenAI assigns its rights
factChecks.excerpt: OpenAI hereby assigns to Customer all OpenAI's right, title, and interest in and to Output.

factChecks.label: Training default
factChecks.value: No training on business data by default
factChecks.excerpt: We don't train our models on your organization's data by default.

factChecks.label: Video endpoint retention
factChecks.value: 48 hours for download, then 30 days abuse monitoring; not eligible for zero data retention
factChecks.excerpt: retains it for 48 hours ... then for 30 days for abuse monitoring.

factChecks.label: Provenance claim
factChecks.value: C2PA plus visible and invisible signals (provider claim)
factChecks.excerpt: Every video generated with Sora includes both visible and invisible provenance signals.

factChecks.label: Sora API lifecycle
factChecks.value: Scheduled discontinuation on September 24, 2026 (consumer app already retired April 26, 2026)
factChecks.excerpt: The Sora API will be discontinued on September 24, 2026.

openItems: Current Sora 2 training-data disclosure: the category-level statement dates to the December 2024 system card, not the sora-2 model family.
API response provenance fields are undocumented; output-behavior testing queued.
Migration deadline: the Sora API is scheduled to end September 24, 2026. Recheck before relying on this surface.

records.serviceSurface: Veo 3.1 via paid Gemini Developer API
records.termsDocument: Gemini API Additional Terms of Service

commercialUse.summary: The paid Gemini API is positioned for professional or business use, but no clause expressly grants commercial use; use remains subject to the API terms and law.

outputOwnership.summary: Non-claim rather than assignment: Google states it will not claim ownership over generated content. Similar content may be generated for others.

trainingUseDefault.summary: Paid services: prompts and responses are not used to improve Google products. Unpaid-tier treatment differs sharply; active Cloud billing determines paid status.

provenanceClaims.summary: Provider claim: Veo output is invisibly watermarked with SynthID, checkable via Google's verification tooling. SynthID is not C2PA; no visible watermark or API provenance field is documented. Not yet output-tested by Vorp Labs.

indemnity.summary: No Google-to-customer output-IP indemnity was verified for the paid Gemini Developer API. Vertex AI or negotiated Google Cloud indemnities do not automatically apply to this surface.

factChecks.label: Output position
factChecks.value: Google will not claim ownership of generated content
factChecks.excerpt: Google won't claim ownership over that content.

factChecks.label: Paid-tier training
factChecks.value: Paid prompts/responses not used to improve products
factChecks.excerpt: Google does not use your prompts ... or responses to improve our products.

factChecks.label: Generated-video retention
factChecks.value: Server-side storage for 2 days
factChecks.excerpt: Generated videos are stored on the server for 2 days.

factChecks.label: Provenance claim
factChecks.value: SynthID invisible watermark (provider claim)
factChecks.excerpt: Videos created by Veo are watermarked using SynthID.

openItems: Customer Input-rights warranty, data residency, and Veo 3.1 training-corpus disclosure remain unverified for this surface.
Veo 2 and Veo 3 model IDs carried a published June 30, 2026 shutdown date; automate alias checks before pinning model IDs.

records.termsDocument: Runway Terms of Use; Runway Enterprise Services Terms

commercialUse.summary: Standard terms do not restrict commercial use of Outputs, subject to compliance; Enterprise ownership terms support commercial deployment under the Order.

outputOwnership.summary: Tier-dependent wording: standard terms say Runway does not claim ownership (a non-claim, not an assignment); Enterprise terms affirmatively state the customer owns Customer Content including Outputs.

trainingUseDefault.summary: Split by tier: standard terms permit training on Inputs and Outputs under a broad perpetual license; Enterprise terms prohibit training on Customer Content, extended to third-party models routed through Runway.

provenanceClaims.summary: Provider claim: all Runway-generated outputs include C2PA metadata, with an earlier claim of invisible watermarks on every generation. Applicability to third-party models in the same catalog is not explicit. Not yet output-tested by Vorp Labs.

indemnity.summary: Standard: none (the customer indemnifies Runway). Enterprise: service-IP indemnity excluding Inputs, misconduct, modification or combination, and Customer Applications; Output-specific coverage is not expressly stated.

factChecks.label: Commercial use (standard)
factChecks.value: Commercial use of Outputs not restricted
factChecks.excerpt: does not restrict ... commercial use

factChecks.label: Training (standard vs Enterprise)
factChecks.value: Standard terms permit training on Inputs and Outputs; Enterprise prohibits it
factChecks.excerpt: used ... for ... model training purposes

factChecks.label: Enterprise ownership
factChecks.value: Customer owns Customer Content
factChecks.excerpt: Customer owns all rights, title, and interest in Customer Content.

factChecks.label: Provenance claim
factChecks.value: C2PA metadata on Runway-generated outputs (provider claim)
factChecks.excerpt: All Runway-generated outputs include C2PA metadata.

openItems: The API marketing page advertises no training on your data, while the standard Terms expressly permit it; the public no-training legal clause is Enterprise-specific.

Lifecycle disclosure is model-specific: gen4_aleph carries a published sunset (2026-07-30, migrate to Aleph 2.0) tracked on the model deprecation tracker, while gen3a_turbo was removed from the catalog without a dated shutdown notice.

records.termsDocument: Luma API Terms of Use (supplementing individual or Enterprise Terms)

commercialUse.summary: API generations may be used commercially per the official API FAQ, unlike non-API Free and Lite platform plans, which are noncommercial and watermarked. Channel is decisive.

outputOwnership.summary: Assigned: customer owns Output and Luma assigns its rights in it, excluding third-party Output.

trainingUseDefault.summary: API Input and Output are not used for training, regardless of plan. The non-API individual platform permits training on both paid and free platform content; it has the opposite default.

provenanceClaims.summary: Conflicting positions: the API FAQ says outputs carry no watermarks of any kind, while the individual Terms reserve the right to add watermarks, content credentials, or provenance metadata. No implemented C2PA or detector verified. Not yet output-tested by Vorp Labs.

indemnity.summary: Self-serve: the customer indemnifies Luma, including API-integration and Output claims. Enterprise terms reference a Luma IP-claim indemnity whose exact coverage was not recovered.

factChecks.label: API commercial use
factChecks.value: API images and videos allowed for commercial use
factChecks.excerpt: Images & Videos created using the API are allowed for commercial use

factChecks.label: API training prohibition
factChecks.value: No training on API Input or Output
factChecks.excerpt: Luma will not use ... Input ... or ... Output ... to train

factChecks.label: Output assignment
factChecks.value: Luma assigns its rights in Output to the customer
factChecks.excerpt: Luma hereby assigns ... interest ... in ... Output.

factChecks.label: Watermark conflict
factChecks.value: API FAQ: no watermarks; individual Terms reserve the right to add provenance measures
factChecks.excerpt: have no watermarks of any sorts

openItems: No fixed API retention duration published; individual terms allow storage-limit changes without notice.
Training-corpus disclosure for current Dream Machine models unverified.

records.termsDocument: TwelveLabs Terms of Use (self-serve); Enterprise Terms of Service + Bonterms AI Standard Clauses v1.0

commercialUse.summary: Enterprise: the customer retains IP in Customer Data (Inputs and Outputs), supporting business use; self-serve terms contain no explicit commercial-use clause.

outputOwnership.summary: Enterprise AI clauses deem Output to be Customer Data, which the customer owns. Embeddings are not expressly defined as Output, so embedding ownership is unresolved.

trainingUseDefault.summary: Opposite defaults by tier: self-serve terms let TwelveLabs train and improve its services on Customer Data; Enterprise AI clauses restrict training to uses solely benefiting the customer.

provenanceClaims.summary: An understanding and indexing surface: watermarks and C2PA generally do not apply to transcript, search, or analysis output, and no lineage or provenance fields for outputs were verified.

indemnity.summary: Self-serve: customer-to-provider only. Enterprise: defense against claims that the Cloud Service infringes IP; no Output-specific indemnity; trials and betas excluded.

factChecks.label: Self-serve training license
factChecks.value: Customer Data may train and improve the services
factChecks.excerpt: train, and improve the Twelve Labs Services

factChecks.label: Enterprise training restriction
factChecks.value: Training only for the customer's benefit
factChecks.excerpt: except solely for the benefit of Customer.

factChecks.label: Enterprise data ownership
factChecks.value: Customer retains IP rights in Customer Data
factChecks.excerpt: Customer retains all intellectual property ... rights in Customer Data

openItems: Embedding ownership unresolved: Outputs are Enterprise Customer Data, but numerical embeddings are not expressly defined as Output.
No complete indexed-video or embedding retention schedule verified; model migrations (Marengo 2.7 to 3.0) require regenerated, incompatible embeddings.

records.termsDocument: No English-language API terms document verified

commercialUse.summary: No English-language, API-specific commercial-use grant was verifiable on the check date; official domains blocked retrieval. Consumer-plan statements must not be transposed to API output.

outputOwnership.summary: No API-specific ownership, assignment, or output-license clause verified.

trainingUseDefault.summary: No primary-source API clause establishing training defaults, opt-outs, or exemptions verified.

provenanceClaims.summary: Visible watermarks observed in consumer products cannot be assumed for API output; no API-specific watermark, C2PA, detector, or provenance field verified.

indemnity.summary: No provider indemnity, eligibility criteria, or exclusions verified.

factChecks.label: Evidence gap
factChecks.value: No English-language Open Platform API terms, retention policy, or provenance documentation was retrievable on the check date

openItems: This evidence gap is the record: procurement teams should obtain and review the governing API terms directly from Kling before deployment.

records.termsDocument: No English developer-API terms verified; retrieved document covers the AI App and Web only

commercialUse.summary: No English-language API-specific commercial-use clause verified. The retrievable MiniMax terms are expressly titled for the AI App and Web, a scope mismatch for the developer API.

outputOwnership.summary: API output ownership unverified. The app/web document grants MiniMax a broad perpetual license over user contributions and generated content, but cannot safely be transposed to the API.

provenanceClaims.summary: No API-specific watermark, C2PA, detector, or provenance field verified.

indemnity.summary: No provider indemnity verified for the API.

factChecks.label: Scope-mismatch trap
factChecks.value: The retrievable English terms govern the MiniMax AI App and Web, not the developer API; the app/web license grant is broad and perpetual
factChecks.excerpt: royalty-free, perpetual, irrevocable, worldwide, non-exclusive right ... and license

openItems: Procurement teams should obtain the governing developer-API agreement from MiniMax directly; the app/web terms are not it.

sources.label: MiniMax AI App and Web Terms

commercialUse.summary: Free users: non-commercial only, with attribution required for publication. Paid subscribers: commercial use permitted subject to the Prohibited Use Policy. Beta services may separately prohibit production use.

outputOwnership.summary: As between the user and ElevenLabs, the user retains rights in generated Output; similar output may be generated for others, so exclusivity is not promised.

trainingUseDefault.summary: Customer Content is licensed for service improvement and development by default. An account-level training opt-out exists but is prospective only; it does not undo prior uses or resulting materials.

provenanceClaims.summary: Provider claim: SynthID audio watermarking covers all free-user TTS generations and selected paid features, with phased expansion; the Audio Detector checks SynthID first, then a statistical classifier. Audio generated before June 2026 carries no watermark. Not yet output-tested by Vorp Labs.

indemnity.summary: No provider-to-customer output-IP indemnity identified in public self-serve terms; the published clause runs the other direction (customer indemnifies ElevenLabs).

factChecks.label: Plan-based commercial rule
factChecks.value: Free non-commercial; paid commercial
factChecks.excerpt: Paid User... may use the Services for commercial purposes

factChecks.label: Output rights
factChecks.value: User retains rights in Output
factChecks.excerpt: as between you and ElevenLabs, you retain all rights in and to your Output

factChecks.label: Training default and opt-out
factChecks.value: Default improvement license; prospective opt-out
factChecks.excerpt: you may opt out of our use of your Content for training at any time

factChecks.label: SynthID rollout
factChecks.value: All free-user TTS plus selected paid features (provider claim)
factChecks.excerpt: applied to all Text to Speech generations by free users and select paid features

factChecks.label: Enterprise deletion
factChecks.value: Enterprise content deleted within 30 days of termination; self-serve deletion discretionary after 180 days of inactivity
factChecks.excerpt: enterprise-level Services... deleted... within thirty (30) days

openItems: Document hierarchy: base Terms (2026-03-31), Agents Terms (2026-04-29), Music terms (2026-05-26), DPA (2026-04-08), and Privacy Policy (2026-05-20) carry separate dates. Check the specific surface.
Agent deployments carry AI-disclosure and call-recording notice duties plus five-year consent-evidence retention under the Agents Terms.

records.termsDocument: ElevenLabs Terms of Service + Prohibited Use Policy + product guides

commercialUse.summary: Same plan rule as TTS (free non-commercial, paid commercial), subject to consent and rights restrictions. Professional Voice Cloning requires the Creator plan or above.

outputOwnership.summary: User retains rights in Output, subject to underlying rights in the sampled voice and the broad content license granted to ElevenLabs.

trainingUseDefault.summary: Voice recordings, voice models, and output fall within the default improvement license, described as perpetual and irrevocable; the training opt-out is prospective only.

provenanceClaims.summary: Clone-generated TTS is covered where it falls within the documented SynthID rollout (all free-user TTS, selected paid features); paid coverage is not universally enumerated.

voiceConsent.summary: Instant: in-product confirmation of rights and consent to clone (with a documentation conflict on whether voice verification applies). Professional: self-voice only; even consent does not let one account create another person's clone. The owner reads a visual CAPTCHA script aloud for voice verification, then may share. Political candidates and elected officials cannot be impersonated regardless of authorization. Under-13 use prohibited; 13-18 requires parental consent.

indemnity.summary: No public provider indemnity; customer indemnity applies.

factChecks.label: PVC self-voice rule
factChecks.value: Only the voice owner can create a Professional Voice Clone
factChecks.excerpt: Even with their consent, you cannot clone someone else's voice

factChecks.label: PVC verification method
factChecks.value: Voice CAPTCHA: owner reads displayed lines aloud
factChecks.excerpt: The image contains several lines of text that the voice owner will need to read out loud and record

factChecks.label: IVC consent basis
factChecks.value: In-product confirmation of right and consent
factChecks.excerpt: confirm that you have the right and consent to clone the voice

factChecks.label: Deletion vs license
factChecks.value: Model deletion does not retract the perpetual license or undo prior training
factChecks.excerpt: You may request deletion of your User Voice Models

openItems: Documentation conflict: the IVC guide shows a checkbox warranty while the API concepts page says both IVC and PVC use voice CAPTCHA. Treat the IVC verification method as unresolved until ElevenLabs clarifies.
Voice Library sharing adds a notice period on removal, and previously generated outputs survive removal.

records.termsDocument: Eleven Music Model-Specific Terms; Music Terms; Music API Terms

commercialUse.summary: A rights matrix, not a yes/no: self-serve plans list online and offline commercial media rights except film, TV, radio, and Studio Games; Free cannot download or stream and requires attribution; Starter cannot stream; reseller and music-library rights are prohibited on self-serve. Enterprise permits all online/offline commercial media use. API access starts at the Starter plan.

outputOwnership.summary: Music output is expressly within the base Output definition, so the user retains rights as between the parties, subject to plan restrictions and non-exclusivity.

trainingUseDefault.summary: Base content license and prospective training opt-out apply to Music Input and Output unless an enterprise agreement says otherwise.

provenanceClaims.summary: No Music-specific SynthID, C2PA, detector, or API provenance field verified; the published Audio Detector rollout expressly covers TTS, not Music.

indemnity.summary: No provider output-IP indemnity; the Music API Terms impose customer indemnity for misuse and content provided to the API.

factChecks.label: Self-serve media rights
factChecks.value: Commercial use permitted except film, TV, radio, and Studio Games
factChecks.excerpt: All online and offline commercial use permitted, except film, TV, radio, & Studio Games

factChecks.label: Artist-prompt prohibition
factChecks.value: Prompts naming artists, songs, labels, or distinctive lyrics prohibited
factChecks.excerpt: Customer is expressly prohibited from submitting... any artist's... real name or stage name

factChecks.label: Artist-voice mimicry ban
factChecks.value: Output may not replicate a recording artist's voice or identifiable characteristics
factChecks.excerpt: shall not prompt Music to generate Output that replicates or mimics the voice... of any recording artist

openItems: Not a voice-clone product, but the Music terms prohibit output that replicates or mimics a recording artist's voice or identifiable characteristics, and prohibit prompts naming artists, songs, albums, labels, or distinctive lyrics.
Marketing describes collaboration with artists, labels, and publishers, but no itemized licensed-work or training-corpus inventory is published.
No official public Suno or Udio API was verifiable on the check date. Unofficial wrapper endpoints are not provider APIs and carry none of these terms.

records.serviceSurface: Audio APIs: TTS, transcription, Realtime, and custom voices
records.termsDocument: OpenAI Services Agreement; OpenAI Service Terms

commercialUse.summary: API customers may integrate the services into customer applications for end users. The non-commercial standalone-audio restriction applies to ChatGPT Voice Output, not API TTS output.

outputOwnership.summary: Customer owns Output as between the parties and OpenAI assigns its rights. For custom voices, ownership of Output does not supply rights in the cloned person's voice or persona.

trainingUseDefault.summary: API customer content is not used to develop or improve OpenAI services unless the customer explicitly agrees.

provenanceClaims.summary: Contractual rather than technical: applications must clearly disclose to end users that the TTS voice is AI-generated. No general API audio watermark, C2PA credential, or public detector verified.

voiceConsent.summary: Custom voices require an uploaded consent recording via an official voice-consent API object with a delete endpoint. The exact script, speaker-match logic, eligibility rules, and whether consent deletion disables derived voices were not verifiable from public docs.

indemnity.summary: Output-IP indemnity for API customers with the standard Service Terms exclusions, including lack of rights in Input.

factChecks.label: ChatGPT Voice vs API
factChecks.value: The non-commercial restriction covers ChatGPT Voice Output, not API TTS
factChecks.excerpt: ChatGPT Voice Output is for non-commercial use only

factChecks.label: AI-voice disclosure duty
factChecks.value: End users must be told the TTS voice is AI-generated
factChecks.excerpt: provide a clear disclosure to end users that the TTS voice... is AI-generated and not a human voice

factChecks.label: Custom-voice consent object
factChecks.value: Consent recording uploaded through a dedicated API resource
factChecks.excerpt: Upload a consent recording that authorizes creation of a custom voice

factChecks.label: Abuse-log retention
factChecks.value: Default abuse-monitoring logs retained up to 30 days
factChecks.excerpt: retained for up to 30 days, unless we are legally required to retain the logs for longer

openItems: Retention of consent recordings, reference recordings, and derived voice objects not published.
Whether deleting a consent object disables every derived voice is unverified.

records.serviceSurface: Gemini API native audio + Lyria music
records.termsDocument: Gemini API Additional Terms of Service

commercialUse.summary: Gemini API use is expressly for professional or business purposes rather than consumer use; generated content may be used subject to the API terms. Lyria model or launch-stage restrictions were not fully captured.

outputOwnership.summary: Google will not claim ownership over generated content, with non-uniqueness and applicable-law caveats.

trainingUseDefault.summary: Unpaid services use inputs and outputs for improvement with possible human review; paid services do not. EEA, Swiss, and UK users receive paid-service data treatment even on unpaid quota.

provenanceClaims.summary: No current model-specific passage establishing SynthID coverage for Gemini API native audio or the Lyria surface was verifiable; a categorical watermark claim should not be published from this evidence.

voiceConsent.summary: No general customer voice-cloning surface verified in the Gemini API audio materials. API users must be 18 or older and may not build clients directed at minors.

indemnity.summary: No Gemini-API-specific provider output indemnity verified; Google Cloud indemnities should not be assumed to apply.

factChecks.label: Business-use framing
factChecks.value: For professional or business purposes, not consumer use
factChecks.excerpt: for developers building with Google AI models for professional or business purposes, not for consumer use

factChecks.label: Unpaid-tier human review
factChecks.value: Unpaid inputs and outputs may be read by human reviewers
factChecks.excerpt: human reviewers may read, annotate, and process your API input and output

factChecks.label: International caching
factChecks.value: Paid-service logs may be stored or cached in any Google facility country
factChecks.excerpt: may be stored transiently or cached in any country in which Google or its agents maintain facilities

openItems: Lyria training-catalogue, licensed-work list, and SynthID coverage for the exact Gemini API surface remain unverified.
Lyria models are preview-stage; preview terms may supplement the Additional Terms.

records.serviceSurface: Cloud TTS, Gemini-TTS, and Chirp 3 Instant Custom Voice
records.termsDocument: Google Cloud Platform Terms + Service Specific Terms + Chirp 3 ICV documentation

commercialUse.summary: Paid Google Cloud services with no surface-specific non-commercial restriction captured; use is governed by the Cloud agreement and, for custom voice, contingent on the mandatory consent workflow.

outputOwnership.summary: Governed by Google Cloud generated-output terms; validate against the customer's governing Cloud agreement per model. This is a different contract stack from the Gemini Developer API.

trainingUseDefault.summary: Google Cloud's business-service rule is that Customer Data is not used to train generalized models without permission; model- and preview-specific exceptions must be checked in the Service Specific Terms.

provenanceClaims.summary: No stock Cloud TTS, Gemini-TTS, or Chirp audio watermark, C2PA credential, detector, or API provenance field verified. Lyria or SynthID claims must not be imported onto this surface.

voiceConsent.summary: Chirp 3 Instant Custom Voice requires a recorded first-person ownership-and-consent statement using Google's exact script (custom scripts not allowed), plus a reference recording. No government-ID or automated speaker-match verification is documented; provider-side revocation and derived-model disablement were not captured.

indemnity.summary: Google Cloud provides contractual IP indemnities in some circumstances, but generated-output coverage per TTS model and launch stage was not verified.

factChecks.label: ICV consent script
factChecks.value: Mandatory first-person ownership and consent statement
factChecks.excerpt: I am the owner of this voice and I consent to Google using this voice to create a synthetic voice model

factChecks.label: No custom consent scripts
factChecks.value: Google's provided statement must be used verbatim
factChecks.excerpt: You can't use a custom consent script... You must use the provided consent statement

factChecks.label: Client-held cloning keys
factChecks.value: Voice-cloning keys stored client-side and supplied per request
factChecks.excerpt: voice cloning keys are stored on the client side and provided per request

openItems: Provider-side retention of consent and reference processing artifacts not captured.
Do not transfer Gemini Developer API data terms onto Cloud TTS or vice versa: separate services, separate contracts.

records.termsDocument: Deepgram Terms of Service + Model Improvement Partnership documentation

commercialUse.summary: Offered as an API business service, but no plan-by-plan commercial-rights clause was verifiable from the public terms on the check date.

outputOwnership.summary: No on-point current transcript or TTS output-ownership clause verified.

trainingUseDefault.summary: The Model Improvement Partnership documentation confirms customer data can participate in model improvement and that opt-out mechanisms exist; a universal account default and every contract exception were not established. Training opt-out and retention are separate controls.

provenanceClaims.summary: STT returns text and metadata, so audio watermarks do not apply to ordinary output; no transcript provenance attestation and no Aura watermark, detector, or provenance field verified.

indemnity.summary: Provider indemnity, eligibility, and exclusions not verified from public materials.

factChecks.label: Model improvement and opt-out
factChecks.value: Customer data may train models; opt-out documented
factChecks.excerpt: Learn about how Deepgram trains our AI models and how you can opt out

factChecks.label: Retention posture
factChecks.value: Flexible retention options claimed; no default duration published
factChecks.excerpt: flexible data retention options

openItems: Default audio and transcript retention durations, residency options, output ownership, input warranty, and indemnity all need a direct terms pass or account-level confirmation.
No public Aura customer voice-cloning workflow verified.

records.termsDocument: AssemblyAI Terms of Service + Privacy Policy + support documentation

commercialUse.summary: No plan-by-plan commercial-use clause verifiable from public terms on the check date.

outputOwnership.summary: No current on-point transcript ownership clause verified.

trainingUseDefault.summary: Contract-dependent: only certain API files, as permitted by the applicable contract, are used for model training, and an opt-out route exists. A provider-wide default should not be inferred.

provenanceClaims.summary: STT returns transcripts and metadata; no transcript authenticity credential or provider-signed provenance field verified.

indemnity.summary: Provider indemnity, eligibility, and exclusions not verified.

factChecks.label: Contract-scoped training
factChecks.value: Only contract-permitted files are used for training
factChecks.excerpt: Only certain files submitted to the API, as permitted by the applicable contract, are used for model training

factChecks.label: Training-file redaction
factChecks.value: Selected training files undergo PII redaction
factChecks.excerpt: These files undergo a redaction process designed to redact personally identifiable information

openItems: Default audio and transcript retention, deletion behavior, residency, ownership, input warranty, and indemnity need a direct terms pass.

changeLog.change: Corrected the Runway record's lifecycle open item: gen4_aleph has a published sunset date (2026-07-30), confirmed against the Runway model catalog. The 2026-07-14 release remains available with the earlier wording.
Initial registry release covering video generation, video understanding, speech, and music provider surfaces.
